zotero.abstractNote: We introduce Progressive Tempering Sampler (PTS), a novel MCMC method that leverages diffusion models to sample from complex, multimodal target distributions. PTS constructs a sequence of tempered distributions by interpolating between a simple base distribution and the target distribution using a diffusion process. By learning a score-based transition kernel that adapts to the tempering schedule, PTS effectively bridges the gap between modes, overcoming the limitations of traditional tempering methods. We demonstrate the efficacy of PTS on a range of challenging sampling tasks, including high-dimensional Bayesian inference and generative modeling, where it consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art samplers.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 42nd International Conference on Machine Learning
